name: getApi
description:
Use this tool whenever you need to fetch data from or send data to any external web service, REST API, or HTTP endpoint. Use it for: looking up information from APIs, calling webhooks, reading data from web services, posting data to remote services.
Authentication options: none (public APIs), apiKey (Bearer token), basic (HTTP Basic), oauth_cc (OAuth2 client credentials — server-wide token), oauth_user (OAuth2 per-user token — uses the token the current user connected at /connections).
DISCOVERY RULES — only when you do not already know the authName:
- For authType "oauth_cc": call getOauthProviders first to discover available provider names.
- For authType "apiKey" or "basic": call getApiBearers first to discover available key names.
- For authType "oauth_user": call getMyConnections first to see which providers the current user has connected. Use the returned name as authName.
Do not guess or invent authName values for oauth_cc, apiKey, or basic.
Returns the parsed response body. Use this instead of asking the user to call the API themselves.

url.description: Full endpoint URL including query parameters if needed.

authType.description: Authentication type. none=no auth, apiKey=Bearer token from key manager, basic=HTTP Basic from key manager (stored as user:pass), oauth_cc=OAuth2 client credentials (server-wide token), oauth_user=OAuth2 per-user token (user must have connected at /connections). Default: none.

authName.description: Secret placeholder name (for apiKey/basic) or OAuth registration name (for oauth_cc/oauth_user). Required when authType is not none.

body.description: Request body as a JSON string. Used with POST, PUT, PATCH.

headers.description: Additional HTTP headers as a JSON object string, e.g. "{\"X-Custom\": \"value\"}".

responseType.description: How to parse the response. auto=detect from content-type, json=always parse as JSON, text=always return as string. Default: auto.
